evals.prompt: create a ticket for adding dark mode to the BudgetWise UI. It should toggle between light and dark themes and remember the user's preference.
evals.expected_output: A complete ticket markdown file saved to d:\repositories\budgetwise\tickets\23-dark-mode.md (or similar number) with: metadata block (Phase, Priority, Depends on, Blocks), Objective section, Frontend Changes with Angular Material theming code, Implementation Notes, Files to Create/Modify, and at least 5 specific Acceptance Criteria. PROJECT-STATUS.md should be updated with the new ticket entry.
expectations: Ticket file is written to d:\repositories\budgetwise\tickets\ with a filename starting with 23 or higher
Ticket contains a metadata block with Phase, Priority, Depends on, and Blocks fields
Ticket contains an Objective section describing the dark mode feature
Ticket contains Angular/TypeScript code snippets showing how to implement theming
Ticket contains at least 5 Acceptance Criteria formatted as - [ ] checkboxes
Ticket mentions localStorage or similar for persisting the theme preference
PROJECT-STATUS.md is updated with a reference to the new ticket

evals.prompt: I want to add CSV export for transactions. Users should be able to click a button on the Transactions page to download all their transactions as a CSV file. Create a ticket for this.
evals.expected_output: A complete ticket markdown file for CSV export functionality. Should include: metadata, objective, frontend changes (button + download logic), and acceptance criteria. Since this is frontend-only (CSV generation can happen client-side), should NOT require backend changes. Should reference the existing TransactionsService at budgetwise-ui/src/app/core/services/transactions.service.ts.
expectations: Ticket file is written to d:\repositories\budgetwise\tickets\ with correct sequential numbering
Ticket contains a metadata block with Phase, Priority, Depends on, and Blocks fields
Ticket references the existing TransactionsService rather than creating a new one
Ticket includes TypeScript code showing how to generate and download a CSV file
Ticket specifies what columns should appear in the CSV output
Ticket contains Acceptance Criteria with at least one criterion about the downloaded file format
Ticket correctly identifies this as frontend-only work (or justifies backend involvement if chosen)

evals.prompt: create a ticket for recurring transactions — users should be able to mark a transaction as recurring (weekly, monthly, yearly) and have it automatically repeat. This needs both backend and frontend work.
evals.expected_output: A complete ticket for recurring transactions. This is complex full-stack work requiring: Prisma schema changes (new fields on Transaction or a new RecurringTransaction model), a backend service/job to create recurring instances, and frontend UI changes to the transaction form. Should include detailed implementation guidance since this touches the most critical service (TransactionsService with balance sync).
expectations: Ticket file is written to d:\repositories\budgetwise\tickets\ with correct sequential numbering
Ticket contains both Backend Changes and Frontend Changes sections
Ticket references the Prisma schema and proposes specific schema changes (new fields or model)
Ticket includes TypeScript/Prisma code snippets for the new schema or service methods
Ticket mentions the critical balance sync concern (Prisma $transaction pattern)
Ticket includes an Implementation Notes section with warnings about the complexity
Ticket contains at least 6 Acceptance Criteria covering both backend API behavior and frontend UI
